# On Liberty

*[Mill](../philosophers/mill.md) · 1859 · *On Liberty**

The great liberal plea for individual liberty. Mill formulates there the 'harm principle': the only legitimate reason to coerce someone against their will is to prevent harm to others; their own good, physical or moral, is never sufficient warrant. Over what concerns only himself, the individual is sovereign.
